scenario_text: A data protection impact assessment was not completed before large-scale biometric processing was launched, data subject rights requests are already backlogged, and the DPO was not consulted before the rollout moved ahead. The event is not a cyber breach yet; it is a privacy-governance and accountability failure around high-risk personal-data processing.

scenario_context.operating_context: High-risk personal-data processing and rollout governance

event_path_summary: High-risk personal-data processing proceeds without DPIA, rights readiness, or DPO consultation.
primary_driver: Weak privacy-governance and accountability controls for high-risk processing
main_business_consequence: The organisation faces regulatory and remediation pressure because privacy-governance controls were not in place before processing began.

valid_risks.title: Privacy governance gap for high-risk processing
valid_risks.why_valid: The event is about DPIA, DPO consultation, and rights readiness rather than an actual disclosure.

valid_risks.title: Privacy-by-design and DPIA failure
valid_risks.why_valid: The scenario explicitly states that the required assessment and governance were not completed.

valid_risks.title: Sensitive personal-data oversight weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: Biometric processing is proceeding without the expected governance safeguards.

invalid_risks.title: Data breach as the primary event
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No actual disclosure or exfiltration has happened yet.

invalid_risks.title: Generic policy breach only
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The event is more specific than a generic policy lapse because privacy-governance controls are explicit.

invalid_risks.title: Cyber intrusion
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The initiating failure is governance, not malicious system misuse.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The primary event path is a privacy-governance and accountability failure around high-risk personal-data processing.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Regulatory implications may follow, but the initiating failure is internal privacy-governance control weakness.
Cyber is not primary because there is no hostile disclosure or intrusion trigger.
Third-Party is not primary because the event is not mainly about supplier or processor exposure.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Collapsing DPIA and DPO wording into generic policy breach instead of a dedicated privacy-governance failure.

scenario_text: Patient-data processing in the UAE proceeds without the required high-risk assessment, medical-records access logging is incomplete, and local safeguards for sensitive data are not clearly documented. The issue is not simply healthcare cyber risk; it is privacy-governance weakness around special-category and health-data handling.

scenario_context.operating_context: Sensitive health-data handling and access governance in UAE operations

event_path_summary: Sensitive health-data processing proceeds without assessment, logging discipline, or clearly documented local safeguards.
primary_driver: Weak governance and oversight for health-data privacy controls
main_business_consequence: The organisation faces regulatory, patient-trust, and remediation pressure because sensitive-data handling controls are not convincingly in place.

valid_risks.title: Patient-data privacy governance gap
valid_risks.why_valid: The event is specifically about governance of health and sensitive personal data.

valid_risks.title: Medical-records access-logging weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: Access logging and oversight are incomplete for a high-risk data class.

valid_risks.title: Sensitive-data safeguard gap in UAE processing
valid_risks.why_valid: Local safeguards are not clearly documented for patient-data handling.

invalid_risks.title: Healthcare cyber breach
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No actual compromise or exfiltration is the stated trigger.

invalid_risks.title: Routine records retention issue
invalid_risks.why_invalid: Retention is not the primary event in the scenario.

invalid_risks.title: Forklift collision injury
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The issue is privacy-governance and safeguarding, not an HSE incident.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The scenario is about sensitive-data privacy governance and access-control readiness, which fits Compliance.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Cyber is adjacent because data security matters, but there is no hostile breach event.
Regulatory concern is downstream of the control weakness.
Legal / Contract is not primary because no contract dispute is described.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Misclassifying patient-data privacy-governance wording as cyber just because medical records are mentioned.

scenario_text: Controller and processor responsibilities are unclear, the record of processing activities is incomplete, and the data processing agreement was not updated before a new personal-data workflow went live. There is no confirmed external exposure yet, but the privacy operating model is no longer clearly defensible.

scenario_context.operating_context: New personal-data workflow and controller-processor accountability model

event_path_summary: A new personal-data workflow goes live before controller-processor governance and processing records are complete.
primary_driver: Weak privacy operating-model governance and accountability controls
main_business_consequence: The organisation faces regulatory and remediation pressure because privacy-accountability evidence is incomplete for the live process.

valid_risks.title: Controller-processor governance gap
valid_risks.why_valid: The event is explicitly about unclear accountability between parties handling personal data.

valid_risks.title: Privacy operating-model weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: The workflow went live without complete governance evidence and records.

valid_risks.title: Record-of-processing and accountability failure
valid_risks.why_valid: RoPA and processing-agreement controls are central to the scenario.

invalid_risks.title: Third-party access compromise
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No malicious third-party compromise is described.

invalid_risks.title: Generic supplier governance issue
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The primary issue is privacy-accountability rather than supplier onboarding or diligence.

invalid_risks.title: Data disclosure incident
invalid_risks.why_invalid: There is no actual leak or unauthorised exposure yet.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The event is a privacy-accountability failure around controller-processor governance and required records.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Third-Party is adjacent because processor arrangements matter, but the event is not inherited control weakness from a partner alone.
Legal / Contract is secondary because the data processing agreement matters, but the primary concern is privacy governance.
Cyber is not involved because there is no attack or breach.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Promoting processor-accountability wording into generic third-party governance instead of privacy-governance failure.

scenario_text: Patient data was transferred across borders for diagnostics support before the required safeguards and transfer assessment were complete, and the receiving team relied on troubleshooting logs that still contained sensitive identifiers. The core event is not a generic healthcare cyber problem; it is cross-border privacy-control failure involving sensitive data.

scenario_context.operating_context: Cross-border diagnostics and support handling for sensitive health data

event_path_summary: Sensitive health data is transferred internationally before required safeguards and assessments are complete.
primary_driver: Cross-border privacy-control failure for sensitive patient data
main_business_consequence: The organisation faces regulatory and remediation pressure because sensitive data moved internationally without defensible safeguards.

valid_risks.title: Cross-border health-data transfer non-compliance
valid_risks.why_valid: The explicit event is international movement of sensitive data without the required safeguards.

valid_risks.title: Sensitive-data transfer-governance failure
valid_risks.why_valid: Transfer controls and assessments were incomplete for patient data.

valid_risks.title: Health-data privacy control failure in overseas diagnostics support
valid_risks.why_valid: The scenario ties patient-data movement to weak diagnostics-support safeguards.

invalid_risks.title: Healthcare cyber intrusion
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No hostile breach or attack is described as the initiator.

invalid_risks.title: Routine privacy-governance only
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The transfer failure is more specific than a general governance weakness.

invalid_risks.title: Simple retention housekeeping issue
invalid_risks.why_invalid: Retention is not the primary event path.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The explicit event path is cross-border movement of patient data without the required safeguards, which is a specific privacy-control failure.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: A broader privacy-governance weakness exists, but the transfer issue is more specific.
Cyber is secondary because no intrusion or exfiltration drives the event.
Regulatory attention is a consequence of the transfer-control failure.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Flattening cross-border sensitive-data transfer wording into generic privacy governance instead of the more specific transfer family.
